methods.name: trosnoth.gamerecording.stats.ServerGameStats.queueSaveWithAttrs

The Django ORM seems to store the ID of foreign key relationships when
the attribute is first set, so if you set the relationship before the
related object is first saved, saving will break. Getting and setting
again just before the save is good enough to make Django happy.

methods.name: trosnoth.trosnothgui.ingame.replayInterface.ViewControlInterface.processEvent
documentation:
Event processing works in the following way:
1. If there is a prompt on screen, the prompt will either use the
event, or pass it on.
2. If passed on, the event will be sent back to the main class, for it
to process whether player movement uses this event. If it doesn't use
the event, it will pass it back.
3. If so, the hotkey manager will see if the event means anything to
it. If not, that's the end, the event is ignored.
Interface for controlling the replay view.

methods.name: trosnoth.trosnothgui.ingame.viewmanager.ViewManager.updateFocus
documentation:
Updates the location that the ViewManager is focused on.  First
calculates where it would ideally be focused, then moves the focus
towards that point. The focus cannot move faster than self.maxSpeed
pix/s, and will only accelerate or decelerate at self.acceleration
pix/s/s. This method returns the negative of the amount scrolled by.
This is useful for moving the backdrop by the right amount.

A ViewManager object takes a given universe, and displays a screenfull
of the current state of the universe on the specified screen object.  This
class displays only a section of the universe and no other information
(scores, menu etc.).

Note: self._focus represents the position that the ViewManager is currently
looking at.  self.target is what the ViewManager should be trying to look
at.

self.target = None - the ViewManager will use its algorithm to follow a
    point of action.
self.target = (x, y) - the ViewManager will look at the specified point.
self.target = player - the ViewManager will follow the specified player.

class_variables.name: trosnoth.gui.framework.menu.MenuDisplay.ACCELERATION
documentation: pix/s/s

Represents the UI side of a menu. To use this kind of element, you should
first create a trosnoth.ui.menu.menu.MenuManager object with appropriate
menus.

Note that a menu manager will show the hotkeys of actions, but will not
actually cause the actions to be executed. For this, you should construct
a gui.hotkey.Hotkeys() object.

For example:

menuMan = MenuManager()
menu1 = Menu(name='file',
             items=[MenuItem('action1', ...),
                    MenuItem('action2', ...),
                    MenuItem('cancel', menuMan.cancel)])
menu2 = Menu(name='main',
             items=[MenuItem('file', lambda: menuMan.show(menu1)),
                    ...])
menuMan.setDefaultMenu(menu2)

menuDisp = MenuDisplay(..., menuMan, ...)

@param  location    should always be an instance of
                    trosnoth.ui.common.Location, but the current
                    implementation accounts for the possibility of it being
                    a point for the top-left.

methods.name: trosnoth.gui.app.PygameRunnerAPI.show_window
documentation:
Shows a Pygame window, and runs an asynchronous Pygame event
loop until either the given coroutine completes, or the Pygame
window is closed by the user.

```
param coroutine

This will be called once the Pygame window is
shown. Once this coroutine completes, the Pygame window will
be closed. If the Pygame window is closed by the user before
this coroutine completes, the coroutine will be cancelled.

param size

If provided, this specifies the size of the Pygame
window. If not provided and a Pygame window has been
previously opened by this Pygame runner, uses the previous
window size.

param full_screen

If provided, controls whether the window
will be shown in full-screen mode. Note that if full_screen
is True and size is not a supported full-screen size, a
suitable size will be automatically chosen.

param scaled

If provided, controls whether the window will be
scaled to be visible on higher resolution monitors. May have
no effect if full_screen is True.

param grab_mouse

If provided, controls whether the mouse will
be constrained to stay within the window.

param target_frame_rate

This value controls the length of
delay between each frame of the event loop. Expressed in
units of frames per second.

param hide_qt

If true, hides all Qt windows until the
Pygame window is closed.

return

the result of coroutine, if it completed.

raises

UserClosedPygameWindow: if the Pygame window was closed
WindowAlreadyOpen: if a Pygame window is already open when

source:31: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

this is called.
```

methods.name: trosnoth.gui.app.PygameRunnerAPI.resize_window
documentation:
Resizes the currently showing Pygame window.

```
param window_size

The new size of the window.

param full_screen

If provided, controls whether the window
will be shown in full-screen mode. If not provided, the
current value will be used.

param scaled

If provided, controls whether the window will be
scaled to be visible on higher resolution monutors.

param grab_mouse

If provided, controls whether the mouse will
be constrained to stay within the window.

param from_event

Should be True iff this call is in response
to a VIDEORESIZE event.

raises

PygameWindowNotOpen: if the Pygame window is not open
```

documentation:
Called to get the Pygame surface used to draw this element. This
will only be called once unless the object changes or is not
used in a frame.

```
param renderer

the DeclarativeRenderer, just in case you need
access to external things like .

param scale

the scale that this element is being drawn at.
This should be multiplied by any length.

return

(surface, origin), where origin is the location of this
element's origin within the returned surface.
```

methods.name: trosnoth.gui.framework.declarative.ComplexDeclarativeThing.build_state
documentation:
Called once when this element wasn't in the previous frame, to
create the initial state object for this element. The state
object can be any mutable object, and is used to store internal
state such as progress through an animation. An empty dict
can be used to ensure that the object is not marked as static,
in order that `draw()` is called each frame. If this method
returns `None` (the default), the object will be marked as
static if in any frame `draw()` does not add any non-static
child elements.

It's important to remember, that the state applies to
this exact element only, and if a new element is created that
does not compare equal to this one, the state object will be
reset.

```
param renderer

the DeclarativeRenderer, just in case you need
access to external things like .

return

typically a dict, but can be any mutable object
```

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.BuyUpgradeMsg

methods.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.BuyUpgradeMsg._processUpgradePurchase
documentation:
Sends the required sequence of messages to gameRequests to indicate
that the upgrade has been purchased by the player.

Signal from interface that a buy has been requested.

In order for everything to stay in sync, an upgrade purchase works in 3
steps.

1. The client sends BuyUpgradeMsg.
2. The server checks that the coins are avaliable, and replies with
    UpgradeApprovedMsg.
3. The client sends PlayerHasUpgradeMsg, which is then broadcast to all
    clients.

To make launching of grenades more responsive, there is a special flag on
Upgrade classes called doNotWaitForServer. If this flag is set, the client
assumes that the purchase is successful unless it hears otherwise, and the
server broadcasts PlayerHasUpgradeMsg immediately at step 2. Step 3 is not
performed in this case.

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.PlayerHasUpgradeMsg
documentation:
Sent by the local client when it receives word from the server that an
upgrade purchase has been approved.

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.PlayerCoinsSpentMsg
documentation:
This message is necessary, because without it a client can't always tell
where the PlayerHasUpgradeMsg pulled all its coins from.

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.CannotBuyUpgradeMsg
documentation: Valid reasonId values are defined in trosnoth.const.

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.UpgradeApprovedMsg
documentation:
Signals to the player that's trying to buy an upgrade that the purchase has
been successful, and the player should proceed to send a
PlayerHasUpgradeMsg to use the upgrade. This back and forth is necessary
because the using of the upgrade needs to happen based on the location of
the player on their own client's screen, not based on the server's idea of
where the client is.

classes.name: trosnoth.messages.upgrades.UpgradeChangedMsg
documentation:
A message for the clients that informs them of a change in an upgrade stat.
statType may be:
    'S' - coin cost
    'T' - time limit
    'X' - explosion radius
    'E' - enabled

modules.name: trosnoth.tools.screenshots
documentation:
trosnoth.tools.screenshots: programmatically generate screenshots

This module generates Trosnoth screenshots using dumped data
representing the Trosnoth world and UI state. It aims to allow
screenshot data to be generated in one version of Trosnoth and used to
build screenshots in later versions with minimal changes to the
screenshot data. To do this, the general philosophy of this module is
that screenshot data should contain what's genuinely necessary to
generate a Trosnoth screenshot, and anything else can be filled in with
reasonable defaults at the time of generating the screenshot.

Screenshot data is generated by GameInterface.get_screenshot_info(). The
simplest way to get this data is to activate globaldebug.enabled and
globaldebug.debug_key_screenshots, then press the debug key (default
middle mouse button) during a game or replay.

  globaldebug.debug_key_screenshots, then press the debug key
  (default middle mouse button) during a game or replay.
param size: the screenshot size
return: a pygame surface
---

methods.name: trosnoth.game.Game.addAgent
documentation:
Connects an agent to this game. An agent may be a user interface,
an AI player, or anything that wants to receive events from the game
and potentially send actions to it.
Every player in Trosnoth must be controlled by an agent and no agent
can control more than one player.
May return a deferred which will fire once the agent is successfully
added.

methods.name: trosnoth.game.Game.playerRemoved
documentation: Called when a player is removed from the game.

The most important interface in the Trosnoth code base is the Game/Agent
interface. A Game represents either a locally running server or a game that
is running on a remote server. In both of these cases, the game will
provide the same interface for agents to connect to for receiving orders
from the game, sending requests, and examining the state of the Universe.

methods.name: trosnoth.game.LocalGame.checkCollisionsWithCollectables

When a player runs into a collectable unit (e.g. a coin or the
trosball), we pay attention not to where we think the collectable units
are, but where the player's client thinks they are. To do this, we need
to project the collectable units back in time based on the player's
current delay.

modules.name: trosnoth.dbqueue
documentation:
Provides two sets of database utilities:
 - dbqueue.add() is used to defer the writing to the database to a
    queue which will be called asynchronously. This should be used with
    asynchronous django DB functions, or combined with
    asgiref.sync.sync_to_async
 - dbqueue.run_in_synchronous_thread runs the given function in a thread
    with no async main loop, to make Django happy. It blocks until the
    function completes

classes.name: trosnoth.levels.base.BotController
documentation:
This class exists as an interface between the level and a PuppetBot. At
the moment it just proxies orders through to the bot, but ultimately the
bot will be running in a separate process.

classes.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.pre_sync_setup
documentation:
Called before all world state is synced to clients. This is
typically used to set up the map, team names, and sometimes team
allocations.

The default implementation selects teams based on the selected
team options. If a scenario is going to generate teams in some
other way, it should override this method. I.e., it should not call
super().pre_sync_setup().

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.run_lobby_period
documentation:
When hosting a LAN game, this is called after the world state is
synced to clients. It should take care of setting up and tearing
down the lobby period, and should call wait_for_ready_players()
or equivalent to wait until everyone selects ready.

The default implementation will return all players to ghosts in
their own territory before returning.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.pre_sync_create_teams
documentation:
Sets team names and players on teams.
Only call this from pre_sync_setup() as it does not sync changes
to clients.

```
param team_info

sequence of (team_name, players) for each team

param neutral_players

if provided, these players will become
neutral

param remove_others

whether or not to remove players who are
not mentioned in team_info or neutral_players (default True)

return

sequence of teams
```

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.tearDownLevel
documentation:
Called when a new level is selected, or the server terminates. This
could be used to tear down event handlers which have been set up.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.run
documentation:
Called when the level starts. By this point the Game and World have
been completely instantiated. When this coroutine returns without any
errors, the level is deemed to be completed, and the server will
return to the lobby.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.findReasonPlayerCannotJoin
trosnoth.levels.base.Level.get_team_to_join
documentation:
When a player asks to join a game, this method is called to
decide which team to put them on, assuming that they are allowed
to join.

```
param preferred_team

the team that the player would like to
join if possible, or None if the player does not care

param user

the authentication server user object if this game
is being run on an authentication server, or None otherwise.

param nick

the player's nick

param bot

whether or not the requested join is from a bot.

return

a team
```

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.getWinner
documentation:
Called when the server is saving the stats record for this game.
Should return the team that has won, or None if the game has drawn.
If the game is still in progress the Level may return None, or may
return who is currently in the lead: this will only ever be used if
the stats are saved half-way through the game and not saved at the
end (e.g. server crashed).

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.addBot
documentation:
Utility function that adds a bot to the game, but bypasses the call
to findReasonPlayerCannotJoin(), because it is this level that has
requested the join. By default, creates a PuppetBot which does
nothing until told.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.addControllableBot
documentation: Adds a PuppetBot to the game, which does nothing until told.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.waitForHumans
documentation:
Utility function that waits until at least number human players have
joined the game, then returns a collection of the human players in
the game.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.playSound
documentation:
Utility function to play a sound on all clients. The sound file must
exist on the client system.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.setUserInfo
documentation:
Utility function to set the tip that all players get to tell them
their current objectives. Note that this function sets this tip
globally for all players.

methods.name: trosnoth.levels.base.Level.notifyAll
documentation: Sends a notification message to all players and observers.

Base class for all standard and custom levels. A level provides
server-only instructions about how a particular game is set up and
operates.

NOTE that clients know nothing about what level is being used, so any
events that affect world state need to be carried out through the
message-passing API in order that clients stay in sync with the server.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.connectZone

Connects the zone at the given location to the zone in the given
direction. The zone at the start location must exist. The final zone

Returns the location of the final zone.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.isConnected
documentation:
Checks whether the given zone is connected in the given direction.
A zone must exist at the given location, but there need not be a
zone at the end location.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.getConnectedDirections
documentation:
Returns all directions which can be directly reached from the zone
at the given location. The zone must exist.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.connect_to_all_neighbours
documentation:
Connects the zone at the given location to all neighbouring
zones. The zone must exist.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.isEverywhereReachable
documentation:
Calculates and returns whether or not every zone in this layout can
be reached from every other zone.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.getReachablesFrom
documentation:
Returns a set of all zone locations that can be reached from the
zone at the given location (including the zone itself).

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.makeEverywhereReachable
documentation:
Opens up zone connections until every zone in this layout can be
reached by every other zone. If the layout consists of separate
partitions of zones that are not even adjacent to one another,
raises a RuntimeError.addZoneAt(a.firstLocation + ZoneStep.NORTHEAST)

methods.name: trosnoth.model.map.ZoneLayout.createMapLayout
documentation:
Creates and returns a MapLayout object that corresponds to this
layout of zones, using appropriate random map blocks.

Represents a distribution of zones on a map, with information about
which zones are connected to which. Does not store information about
actual terrain etc. beyond whether transitions between adjacent zones are
blocked or open.

When a ZoneLayout() is newly constructed, it has a single zone, located
at z.firstLocation.

If symmetryEnforced is True, this zone layout will be symmetrical around
the column in which the first zone is located.

This may represent a user interface, an AI player, someone connecting over
the network, or anything else that wants to receive interact with the game.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerDriver.reset
documentation:

after a resync or a world reset.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerDriver.game_paused

methods.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerDriver.game_resumed

methods.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerDriver.world_ticked
documentation: This is called whenever the underlying world processes a tick.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerDriver.ui_tick
documentation:
This may be called to indicate that the UI has advanced one
frame, with a time of delta_t since the last frame.

It must return a tuple of (player_tick, player_fraction) for
use when drawing the local player in the UI.

Note that some concrete agents (e.g., AIAgent) might never call
this method.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.agent.ConcreteAgent
documentation:
Base class for Agents that actually represent the world (rather than
proxying it through to the Network or some other agent).

Note that a concrete agent is designed to represent only zero or one
players.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.agent.DiscreteLocalDriver
documentation: For bots and other situations where a smooth UI isn't necessary.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalPlayerSmoother
documentation:
For the UI. If world ticks come too slowly, this driver gradually
slows down the rate of local player ticks until they catch up again.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.agent.LocalState
documentation:
Stores state information which a client wants to keep which do not need to
wait for a round trip to the server. e.g. when a player moves, it should
start moving on the local screen even before the server has received the
message.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.unit.Advanceable
documentation:
Useful for editor type-checking: these things can be treated as
units by the system that moves units each tick.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.unit.InteractingUnit
documentation:
For use by units that interact with players based on the player’s
position on the server, e.g., shots and mines.

Not for things like Trosball and coins which interact based on the
player’s client position.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.unit.Unit.reset

methods.name: trosnoth.model.unit.CollectableUnit.collidedWithPlayer

automatically happen on clients too. Therefore, it's a good idea to
just send a message, and let the message handler do the hard work here.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.unit.CollectableUnit.collidedWithLocalPlayer

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.updateState
documentation:
Update the state of this player. State information is information
which is needed to calculate the motion of the player. For a
human-controlled player, this is essentially only which keys are
pressed. Keys which define a player's state are: left, right, jump and
down.
Shooting is processed separately.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.lookAt
documentation:
Changes the direction that the player is looking.  angle is in
radians and is measured clockwise from vertical.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.getGroundIntentionAndDirection
documentation:
Returns intent, direction where direction is -1 for left and +1 for
right, and intent is -1 for slow down, 0 for walk and 1 for run.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.getCurrentVelocity
documentation: Used to decide how fast the Trosball should go if it's dropped.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.applyVelocityThresholdsAndAcceleration
documentation:
Adjust the player's velocity for one tick.
If this player is initiating a jump, run, or push off a wall,
this method applies the appropriate initial instantaneous velocity
minimum to the player.
This method then applies one tick's worth of this player's current
acceleration based on gravity and the keys pressed.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.processJumpState
documentation:
Checks the player's jump key state to decide whether to initiate a jump
or stop a jump.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.getMinisculeCollision
documentation:
Returns the collision that would occur if this player moved a small
distance (default 0.1 units) in the direction of the given vector.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.isPullingAgainstGrapplingHook
documentation:
Return True iff the player is pressing the left or right arrow key that
would pull away from where the grappling hook is attached.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.constrainVelocityByGrapplingHook
documentation:
If this player has a grappling hook currently attached to something,
this method will update the player's velocity to ensure that the
player is being pulled towards the grappling hook's anchor point at
the appropriate velocity.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.constrainVelocityToSurface
documentation:
If this player is touching a surface, this method will make sure
that the player's current velocity does not push the player through
the surface.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.getPlayerMotion
documentation:
---
return: |-
  (finalPos, collision) for moving one tick's worth of
  distance based on this player's current velocity.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.applyVelocity
documentation:
Applies one tick's worth of the player's current velocity to the
player's position. If there is an obstacle in the player's path,
only moves the player as far as the obstacle, and zeroes any
component of the velocity that goes into the surface of the obstacle.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.discretiseLocation
documentation:
In order for the bot motion to work, the map needs to have a finite
number of stationary locations. This method looks up whether there's a
canonical stationary location near the player's current location,
and moves them there if the player is stationary.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.player.Player.advance
documentation:
Called by this player's universe when this player should update
its position. deltaT is the time that's passed since its state was
current, measured in seconds.

is wrapped with @delay_so_messages_will_apply_immediately.

modules.name: trosnoth.model.hub
documentation:
The hub/node interface exists primarily for sending and receiving network
messages, but it is also used when Trosnoth is run in isolated mode (for
testing).
This interface provides a channel for messages to be passed between agents and
a game. A hub sits on the game side of this interface, while a node sits on the
agent side. The interface may be used to pass messages for zero or more agents.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.hub.Hub

methods.name: trosnoth.model.hub.Hub.connectNewAgent
documentation:
Requests that a new agent be connected to the game that this hub
represents. If successful, this method will return an agentId for the
new agent (or a deferred that results in the agentId).
May raise UnableToConnect or Unauthorised.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.hub.Hub.disconnectAgent
documentation:
Requests that the agent with the given id be disconnected from the
game. May return a deferred that will not complete until the agent is
disconnected.
Note that the onAgentDisconnected event will fire whenever an agent is
disconnected, regardless of whether it is triggered by this function or
some other reason (e.g. lost network connection).
Sits on the game side of the hub/node message passing interface.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.hub.UnableToConnect
documentation: Raised by connectNewAgent if the connection could not be established.

classes.name: trosnoth.model.hub.Unauthorised
documentation: Raised by connectNewAgent if the user is not allowed to connect.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.zonemechanics.ZoneCaptureCalculator.get_current_capture_info
documentation:
Checks to see whether the room is being captured this tick. If
not, return None. Otherwise, return a dict with information
about the capture.

The returned dict contains:
    team - the team that should now own the zone (None if zone
        neutralised due to multiple simultaneous tags)
    player - the player who captured the zone (None if zone
        neutralised due to multiple simultaneous tags)
    defenders - players in the zone who are on the defending team
    attackers - players in the zone who are on the team(s) who
        tagged the zone

contributed to the neutralisation of this sector. Usually this will
just be a single zone, but in some rare cases multiple captures
during the some tick will have together caused the neutralisation.

Uses the zone neutralisation logic to calculate how many zone points an
enemy team would gain by capturing this zone. That is, 2 points for the
zone itself, plus one for each zone neutralised in the process.

Contains all the methods for performing logic about whether zones are
capturable and what happens if they are captured.

functions.name: trosnoth.qtreactor.install_async_qt_reactor
documentation:
Installs the asyncio/Qt/Twisted main loop. This will not work if a
different Twisted reactor has already been installed.

functions.name: trosnoth.qtreactor.declare_twisted_reactor_was_intentionally_chosen
documentation:
Use this if you need to import a module that calls
declare_this_module_requires_qt_reactor, but you don't want to
install the asyncio/Qt/Twisted main loop.

functions.name: trosnoth.qtreactor.declare_this_module_requires_qt_reactor
documentation:
This should be called before any direct or indirect Twisted imports
in any module that requires the asyncio/Qt/Twisted main loop to be
installed.

If declare_twisted_reactor_was_intentionally_chosen() has been
previously called, this call will do nothing.
Otherwise, this function will install the asyncio/Qt/Twisted main
loop if possible, and raise RuntimeError otherwise (e.g., if a
different Twisted reactor has already been installed.)

functions.name: trosnoth.qtreactor.declare_this_module_requires_asyncio_reactor
documentation:
This should be called before any direct or indirect Twisted imports
in any module that requires the asyncio/Twisted main loop to be
installed, but does not require Qt.

If declare_twisted_reactor_was_intentionally_chosen() has been
previously called, this call will do nothing.
Otherwise, this function will install the asyncio/Twisted main
loop if possible, and raise RuntimeError otherwise (e.g., if a
different Twisted reactor has already been installed.)

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionShape.checkConcavePolygonCollision
documentation:
Checks whether the a unit of this shape travelling along the given

```
param start

the polygon to test against

return

a Collision object, or None for no collision
```

```

the start position of the unit

the (deltaX, deltaY) that the unit is moving

param polygon

the open polygon to test against

return

a Collision object, or None for no collision
```

Abstract base class for shapes to be used for collision testing against

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.checkLedgeCollision
documentation:

```
the start position of the unit
```

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.getMotionBoundaries
documentation:
---
param start: the start position of a unit of this shape
param vector: the (deltaX, deltaY) the unit is moving
return: iterable of axes representing the edges of this motion path.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.getSurfacePointFromTangent
documentation:
---
param origin: the centre of the ellipse
param tangent: a vector representing the direction of the tangent
return: |-
  the point where a tangent of that slope would touch the
  ellipse.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.getEdgeCollision
documentation:
---
param polygon: a LeafNode representing the closed polygon to test
param start: the start position of a unit of this shape
param vector: the unit's attempted displacement vector
return: |-
  (dist, contactPoint, angle) for the greatest distance the
  unit can travel and still have a separating axis, or (None,
  None, None) if the entire displacement vector still is separated
  by an axis along one of the polygon edges.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.getMaxMotionToAxis
documentation:
---
param start: the start position of a unit of this shape
param vector: the (deltaX, deltaY) that the unit is trying to move
param x1, y1: defines the first point of the axis to test
param x2, y2: defines the second point of the axis to test
return: |-
  (dist, contactPoint) where:
  dist: is the maximum distance the unit could travel in this

  > direction and still lie entirely outside the axis defined by
  > the given points.

  contactPoint: is the point of collision with the axis.

  If the unit is already inside the axis, returns (-0.05, None),
  so that collisions touching the surface are distinguishable from
  those where the unit is already inside the axis.
  If even an infinite motion would leave the unit outside the
  axis, returns (None, None).
---

Note that the parameter vector is only used to to determine the
direction of motion, but its magnitude is ignored, so this method
may return a distance greater than the length of vector.

methods.name: trosnoth.model.physics.CollisionEllipse.getVertexCollision
documentation:
Calculates the greatest distance this shaped unit would move
along the given path before colliding with a vertex of the given
polygon.

```
param start

the starting point of the unit

param vector

the direction of motion

param points

the points of the polygon

param closed

True for a closed polygon, False for open

return

(dist, vertex, tangentAngle), where
dist: the distance moved, or None if none of the vertices have a

source:11: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

separating axis that aligns with the tangent of the ellipse
(that is, none of the vertices can be hit without first hitting
an edge).

source:14: (WARNING/2) Block quote ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

vertex: the point of collision
tangentAngle: the angle of the tangent at the point of collision
```

Note that dist may be negative, in cases where the ellipse has passed
the polygon already.

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.utils.UIScreenRunner
documentation:
Provides an awaitable interface for a UI screen that's built using
an event-driven system. The caller should await the run() method,
and the UI screen should link completion events (e.g. 'Ok' and
'Cancel' buttons) to the done() method.

Does not allow reentrant calls.

NOTE: In many cases it's simpler to just use a Future instead of
a UIScreenRunner. The only thing a UIScreenRunner offers over a
bare Future is the ability to start it again after it's finished.

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.message.MessageBase.handler
documentation:
Decorator for methods which handle this message type, particularly
useful when defining MessageConsumer subclasses.

Allows message classes to be defined by:
    class CustomMessage(Message):
        fields = ('myField', 'otherField')
And then instantiated by:
    x = CustomMessage(17, otherField=2)
Which will create an object where:
    x.myField == 17
    x.otherField == 2

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.netmsg.NetworkMessage.pack

Called by buildMessage() to build an instance of this class from a

class_variables.name: trosnoth.utils.netmsg.NetworkMessage.trace
documentation: Set to True to see where a message goes

Allows network messages to be defined by:
    class CustomNetMsg(NetworkMessage):
        fields = 'playerId', 'msgId', 'comment'
        packspec = 'cB*'
        idString = 'Cust'
Parameters are as follows:
    fields - as with Message class
    packspec - a struct.pack string, except that network byte order will be
        automatically used (do not specify byte order), and the last
        character may optionally be * meaning that the rest of the message
        is considered to be a single string.
Network message class

classes.name: trosnoth.welcome.play.UIRoutinesAPI
documentation:
This class exists to help with editor type suggestions. It defines
the function signatures of the various attributes of UIRoutines.

classes.name: trosnoth.welcome.play.RateLimiter
documentation:
Limits calls to ping() to a maximum of number calls per period
seconds. Any additional calls will asynchronously wait until the
period is over.

If ping() is called while another call to ping() is waiting, there
is no guarantee as to the order in which the two calls will return.

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.lifespan.LifeSpan
documentation:
Used by anything which may end at some point (e.g., level, trigger,
server). Allows registration of things which should also be torn down
when this LifeSpan ends.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.gui.align
documentation:
Aligns the position of the given DirectGUI element based on the given
parameters.

midX and midZ specify the location of the centre of the element, while x
and z specify the location of the origin of the element.

modules.name: trosnoth.utils.profiling
documentation: Based on https://people.gnome.org/~johan/lsprofcalltree.py
lsprofcalltree.py: lsprof output which is readable by kcachegrind

Johan Dahlin
Modified by Joshua D Bartlett

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.twist.WeakCallLater
documentation:
Calls twisted.internet.reactor.callLater, but only maintains a weak
reference to the object whose method is to be called. If the object is
garbage collected, the callLater is cancelled.

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.twist.WeakLoopingCall
documentation:
Wraps a twisted.internet.task.LoopingCall, but only maintains a weak
reference to the object whose method is to be called. If the object is
garbage collected, the looping call is stopped.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.collision.collideTrajectory
documentation:
Returns true if pt lies within a distance of tolerance from the
line segment described by origin and trajectory.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.event.waitForEvents
documentation:
Utility function that waits for the first of a number of given events to
trigger. Returns (event, args), indicating which event fired, and the
arguments that it fired with.

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.event.Event

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.event.Event.wait
documentation:
Returns a Deferred that waits for the given event to fire,
and returns a dict of the parameters received by the call. This
requires that the event was initialised with a signature.

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.event.Event.wait_future
documentation: Like wait(), but returns an asyncio.Future.

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.event.Event.waitOrRaise
documentation:
Requires that this event was instantiated with a signature of only
one parameter. If this parameter is given a Failure, the returned
deferred  will errback, otherwise it will callback with the single
parameter as its result.

If provided, signature is a sequence of strings indicating the names of
arguments to the event calls.

documentation: Compares two floating point values for approximate equality.

modules.name: trosnoth.utils.twistdebug
documentation:
Provides extra debugging about which reactor calls are taking a long time.

Note that this adds the overhead of two system clock calls per function call
that the reactor makes.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.twistdebug.timed_add_callbacks
documentation: Replaces Deferred.addCallbacks so that callbacks are timed.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.twistdebug.timed_delayed_call
documentation: Replaces DelayedCall.__init__ so that delayed calls are timed.

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.twistdebug.FunctionTimer

methods.name: trosnoth.utils.twistdebug.FunctionTimer.wrap
documentation:
Wraps the function so that when it is called, its execution will be
timed with this timer.

Used to time functions and log those which take longer than a particular
threshold. If an inner function is also being logged, this is not counted
towards the outer function's total.

This is useful for example when deferreds are called back - any queued
callbacks are called immediately, within the d.callback() call, but if
they're wrapped it won't be counted towards the caller's total.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.sirrobin.BravelyRunAway
documentation:
He was not at all afraid to be killed in nasty ways,
Brave, brave, brave, brave Sir Robin!

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.sirrobin.RespawnFarAway
documentation: Respawns somewhere away from enemies.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.cancel_task
documentation:
Coroutine which cancels the given task and does not return until the
task finishes all cleanup from being cancelled.

```
param task

a task that has not already been awaited
```

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.all_completed
documentation:
Like asyncio.gather(), except for a few major differences:
: 1. the awaitables are passed in a sequence rather than as
     : variable-count arguments
  2. any exceptions in the awaitables are caught and logged, and
     : a return value of None is used in the returned sequence.

```
param awaitables

a sequence of coroutines or tasks to wait for.
```

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.create_safe_task
documentation:
Like asyncio.create_task(), except that any exceptions raised by the
coroutine will be caught and logged, and the task will have a return
value of None.

```
param aw

an awaitable

return

a Task
```

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.single_entry_task
documentation:
Decorator which indicates that the decorated asynchronous method can
only be running once at a given time. If the method is called when
it's already running, the first call will be cancelled.

functions.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.delay_so_messages_will_apply_immediately
documentation:
Decorator that makes the decorated function or method wait for the
next iteration of the main loop before running. This can be useful
server-side because it ensures that messages sent from inside the
wrapped function or method will be applied immediately (rather than
potentially being deferred in sendServerCommand() because processing
for another message is not yet complete).

classes.name: trosnoth.utils.aio.SimpleAsyncIterator
documentation:
Callback-based code can return this so that the caller can use it in
an async for statement.

To push a value to the caller, use .next(value). To close the
iterator, call .done().

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.john.JohnBot
documentation:
This is not the original JohnAI, as the bot API has changed substantially
since JohnAI was written. Instead, this bot is inspired by version 1.1.3 of
JohnAI, last edited 2010-12-28. It maintains the following behaviours of
the original JohnAI:
 - Chases and shoots at nearby enemies.
 - Uses grenades when there are 3 or more enemies in the zone

modules.name: trosnoth.bots.orders
documentation:
This module contains the classes used by the main Bot base class in order to
follow orders like moving to an orb or attacking an enemy.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.BotOrder
documentation: Base class for the simple bot orders.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.StandStill
documentation: This is the default order. It does not tell the bot to do anything.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.MoveGhostToPoint
documentation:
Points the ghost towards the given point, stopping when it reaches that
point or when it enters the given zone, whichever happens first.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.PathFindingOrder

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.PathFindingOrder.finishedRoute

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.PathFindingOrder.isRouteGoodEnough

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.PathFindingOrder.getTargetPos
documentation:
Subclasses should override this to provide the position that we're
currently aiming for.
Base class for orders that need to do path finding through the map.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.MoveToOrb
documentation:
Moves the given live player towards the given orb, stopping after touching
the orb, or upon entering the zone if stopOnEntry is True.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.RespawnInZone
documentation:
If the specified zone was not respawnable when the order was given, waits
until it is. If it was but the zone becomes unrespawnable, the order is
considered completed.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.FollowPlayer

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.FollowPlayer.getTargetPos
documentation:
Subclasses should override this to provide the position that we're
currently aiming for.

Follows the given target player until that player dies or this player dies.
If attack is True, shoots at the player.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.MoveToPoint.getTargetPos
documentation:
Subclasses should override this to provide the position that we're
currently aiming for.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.CollectTrosball

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.orders.CollectTrosball.getTargetPos
documentation:
Subclasses should override this to provide the position that we're
currently aiming for.
Moves towards the trosball until someone picks it up or this player dies.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.GoalSetterBot

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.GoalSetterBot.showGoalStack
documentation: For debugging.

Base class for bots which mainly operate by setting high-level goals, which
may themselves set subgoals.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.start
documentation: Called when this goal should begin its work.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.stop
documentation: Should disable any active components of this goal.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.setSubGoal
documentation:
If the given goal is already the current sub-goal, does nothing.
Otherwise, stops the current sub-goal, and starts the given one.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.returnToParent
documentation:
Call this method to tell the parent goal that this goal is either
completed, or no longer relevant.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.returnedFromChild
documentation:
Called by child's returnToParent() method. The default implementation
checks that the returning child is this goal's subgoal, then calls
reevaluate().

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.Goal.reevaluate
documentation:
Called by the default implementations of start() and
returnedFromChild() to determine what this goal should do next.
Represents something that the bot is trying to achieve.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.MoveIndirectlyWithinZoneBase
documentation:
Base class for goals which move indirectly within a zone.
Aborts if the player dies or leaves the zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.MessAroundInZone
documentation:
Wander around in the zone the player is in at the time of construction.
Aborts if the player dies or leaves the zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.MeanderTowardsOrb
documentation:
Move in the general direction of the orb and stay in the orb's
general vicinity. Aborts if the player dies or leaves the zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.RespawnNearZone

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.goalsetter.RespawnNearZone.zoneTagged
documentation: We may be waiting to respawn in a zone that's just changed ownership.
Respawns in a zone that's as close as possible to the given zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.terminator.KillThings
documentation: Just kill things. Forever.

functions.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.friendly_zone_is_threatened
documentation:
---
param i_am_there: true if I am already there
param my_team: the team of this bot
param sub_player: see sub_player docs in DynamicZoneLogic.get_living_players_by_team
param zone: a zone
return: |-
  true iff I need to be in this zone to stop it being
  capturable
---

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.DecideBetweenSilverOrRangerAlgorithm
documentation:
If this is not a regular Trosnoth match, fall back to RangerBot
algorithm.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.WinSoloTrosnothGame

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.WinSoloTrosnothGame.reevaluate
documentation: Decide whether to stay in the current zone, or move to another.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.WinSoloTrosnothGame.will_returning_help
documentation:
---
param zone: a threatened zone that this player is not in
return: |-
  true iff adding this player to the zone would stop it
  from being capturable.
---

The aim of SilverBot is to win a 1v1 Trosnoth game on a map that's
only one zone high at all points.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.CaptureCurrentZone
documentation:
Captures the zone the bot is currently in. Assumes that the parent
goal already has triggers in place that will notice when the zone
changes hands or the bot dies.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.AdvanceToCapturableZone
documentation:
Selects a neighbour of the bot's current zone which is not owned by
the bot's team and moves to it. Gives preference to rooms which are
owned by enemy teams over neutral rooms.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.AdvanceToFrontLine
documentation:
Selects a neighbour of the bot's current zone which is on the front
line. That is, it is not owned by an enemy team, but is adjacent to
a zone which is. Gives preference to rooms which are contested.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.MoveToZone
documentation: Moves to the given zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.EngageWithEnemiesInZones
documentation:
Tries to kill enemies without leaving the specified rooms. Assumes
that the parent goal has triggers to notice when the player dies
or succeeds in killing enough enemies.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.MoveTowardsEnemy
documentation:
Moves towards the given player. Assumes that the parent goal has
triggers to check when either player dies.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.silver.AvoidEnemiesInZones
documentation:
Moves around, trying to avoid being within firing range of enemies,
while staying in the given rooms.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.ReachCurrentObjective
documentation: Notices what the current level's botGoal is and tries to achieve that goal.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.RunAway
documentation:
Used when this is the last rabbit alive. Selects the zone furthest from
the player and moves to it.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.WinStandardTrosnothGame

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.WinStandardTrosnothGame.reevaluate
documentation: Decide whether to stay in the current zone, or move to another.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.WinStandardTrosnothGame.getZoneUtility
documentation: How useful would it be to be in this zone?
Win the current game of Trosnoth by capturing all the zones.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.CaptureZonesButDoNotDie
documentation:
This bot goal is used for villagers in the space vampire game mode.
Bots with this goal are cautious, but do capture zones if it's safe.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.CaptureZone
documentation:
Respawns if necessary, moves to the given zone, messes around until it's
capturable, and captures it. If the player dies, respawns and tries again.
Returns if the zone is captured or becomes uncapturable by virtue of having
no adjacent zones owned by the team.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.DefendZone
documentation:
Respawns if necessary, moves to the given zone and messes around there.
If the player dies, respawns and continues. Returns if the zone is
captured or neutralised.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ranger.KillEnemyInZone
documentation:
Tries to kill the nearest enemy while staying in the current zone.
Completes if the number of players in the zone changes. Aborts if the
player dies or leaves the zone.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.puppet.PuppetBot

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.puppet.PuppetBot.orderFinished
documentation:
Called by an order when it is complete or cannot continue further. May
be overridden by subclasses.

Does nothing except when order methods (e.g. standStill(), move_to_orb()
etc.) are manually called.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.pathtest.PathTestBot
documentation:
Chooses a human player. When that player shoots, the bot moves to where
that player is.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.ordertest.OrderTestBot
documentation:
Not a very sensible bot, just exists to test the system of giving bots
orders.

functions.name: trosnoth.bots.base.get_available_bot_names

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.base.AIAgent

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.AIAgent.getBot
documentation:
@return: a Deferred which fires with this agent's Bot object,
    as soon as it has one.
Base class for an AI agent.

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.applyStateChanges
documentation:
Sends messages to adjust this player's key state according to the
given state changes. This is a utility method designed to be used by
the bot movement system. Calling it directly from bot subclasses may
interfere with bot motion and pathfinding.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.releaseMovementKeys
documentation:
Updates the player's key state so that it is not pressing any of the
keys. This method should not be called directly by subclasses. It
should only be called by the bot movement system, otherwise it may
interfere with other bot motion. To order the bot to stand still,
use standStill() instead.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.orderFinished
documentation:
Called by an order when it is complete or cannot continue further. May
be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.setOrder
documentation:
Starts the given order. Generally, bots should use the methods below in
preference to this one.
==== Order interface ====

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.standStill
documentation:
Orders the bot to stop moving. If it's in the air, it will complete its
current jump / fall motion.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.moveToPoint
documentation: Orders the bot to move as close as possible to the given point.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.move_to_room
documentation: Orders the bot to move into the given room.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.move_to_orb
documentation: Orders the bot to touch the orb of the given room.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.attackPlayer
documentation: Orders the bot to hunt down and kill the given player.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.followPlayer
documentation:
Orders the bot to get as close as possible to the given player and stay
there until either the player dies, or another order is given.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.collectTrosball
documentation: Orders the bot to move towards the trosball.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.respawn
documentation: Orders the bot to respawn, with an optional zone to move to first.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.setAggression
documentation:
Determines whether the bot will, while carrying out its other orders,
shoot at any enemies it sees.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.set_dodges_bullets
documentation:
Determines whether the bot will attempt to avoid enemy bullets
@param dodges_bullets: Boolean

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.set_upgrade_callback
documentation:
Calls the given callback delay after the bot has cost money for
delay seconds. Clears any previously set upgrade callback.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.setUpgradePolicy
documentation:
Tells this bot how to approach purchasing upgrades. This setting
persists until it is changed with another call to this function.

upgrade: which upgrade to purchase, or None to disable buying
coinBuffer: how many surplus coins should there be before purchasing
delay: how long should the bot wait after there are enough coins

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot._playerDied
documentation: ==== End order interface ====

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.playerResynced
documentation:
Called when the current player's position jumps due to a resync
message.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.base.Bot.worldReset
documentation:
Called when the current player's position jumps due to a world reset
message.

functions.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.buildWorldAndBlockDefForSingleLayout
documentation:
Creates a dummy world containing the given map block layout for simulation.

```
return

(world, blockDef)
```

functions.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.shiftIndices
documentation:
---
param direction: the direction to move
param indices: |-
  the map block indices in the same order that they're
  used to index map layout.blocks (i.e., (row index, column index))
return: |-
  the indices of the map block in the given direction from the
  original indices
---

functions.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.exhaustiveDijkstra
documentation:
Performs Dijkstra's algorithm exhaustively over the search space.

```
param visitVertex

a function that is called with parameters (vertex,
cost), indicating the minimum cost from the start vertex to the given
vertex.

param expandVertex

see BasicAStar()

param startVertices

iterable of (vertex, startCost) for vertices to
start search from.

return

```

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingAction
documentation: Represents a possible action for player path-finding.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.dropRegion
documentation: Drops all data related to the given region and its combinations.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.allRegionCombos
documentation: Returns a set of all valid combinations of regions in self.regions.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.getRegionCombosFrom
documentation:
Iterates through all valid region combos that transition in the
given direction from the given region (not mirrored).

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.collisionPointIsBorderline
documentation:
---
return: |-
  True iff the given offset is very close to a boundary
  between locKey indices, and therefore could be susceptible to
  floating point rounding errors.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.calculateTransitionsFromSinglePoint
documentation: For debugging.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.getNewComboEdges
documentation:
Iterates through (point, path, flipPath) tuples for all starting points
that aren't yet in the database for this PathFindingRegionCombination.
This includes edges from the adjacent regions, as well as border
points.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.addInternalTransition
documentation:
Useful for manually adding transitions to the database in order to
try to reach a location which didn't become reachable by the random
transitions during general database building.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.plotAction
documentation: For debugging.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.plotComboAction
documentation: For debugging.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.tryNewComboEdges
documentation:
Useful if a combo point doesn't have any outgoing edges. Tries to
find new routes out of here.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.calculateSingleWaypointTransitionCosts
documentation:
Fills in all transitions costs needed in order for a path to be
followed from the given waypoint to the closest waypoint in the
other half of the given combo.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.findPossibleCostsForWaypointTransition
documentation:
Uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find the cost to get to dest for all
points that are both in the same half of the combo as src,
and are closer to dest than src is.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStoreBuilder.fillNecessaryCostsForWaypointTransition
documentation:
Uses the costs calculated by findPossibleCostsForWaypointTransition
and saves them in the combo, but only if they can be reached in a
downhill walk from src.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.TwoBlockActionProcessor.simulationAdvanced
documentation:
Use pointData to keep track of how many ticks passed before the
player passed into the other region.

class_variables.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingStep.end
documentation: type: StationaryPoint

classes.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRoute

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRoute.isFinished
documentation:
---
return: True iff the player has already reached the end of this route.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRoute.truncateAtNextOpportunity
documentation:
Causes this route to be truncated as soon as the player reaches a
stationary position.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRoute.started
documentation:
We don't know whether applyOneFrame() will be called the same tick
as this, or next tick, so it used to be essential to make the fall
predictable - this is no longer strictly necessary, but doesn't hurt.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRoute.attemptLocalPathTo
documentation:
Attempts to extend this path to the given point using a heuristic
based on common waypoints. This will only work if the points are
sufficiently close to have waypoints in common.

```
param destination

the target point
```

Represents a route that a bot player uses to navigate the map. When
created, represents an empty route from the player's current position.
Use extendTo() to make the route non-empty.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.BasicAStar.__init__
documentation:
---
param checkSolution: |-
  a function accepting a vertex and returning
  True or False depending on whether the given vertex is a
  solution point.
param expandVertex: |-
  a function accepting a vertex and returning an
  iterable of (edgeCost, edgeData, newVertex), where edgeCost is
  the cost of an edge from vertex to newVertex, and edgeData is
  any arbitrary data that is associated with the edge. The value
  of edgeData is retrieved as part of the result of a call to
  getData().
param heuristic: |-
  a function accepting a vertex and returning the
  heuristic estimate of the cost from the given vertex to the
  solution.
---

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.BasicAStar.getPath
documentation:
Shows the path to the given or current vertex from the start vertex
of the search.

```
param vertex

if provided, the vertex to show the path to. If not
provided, the vertex of the current iteration is used.

return

(vertices, edges) where vertices is a list of all vertices
in the path (including the start and end vertices), and edges is
a list of the edgeData for each edge in the path.
```

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRegion.addDepartingEdge
documentation:
Records edges that depart from this region. This is not useful at
run-time, but is useful for calculating transitions between this region
and others.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRegion.addNearbyRoute
documentation:
Records routes to get near to parts of the map which may not have
stationary points in them.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRegionCombination.getDirectionRelativeToTransition
documentation:
From the perspective of the world where this transition is in its
canonical orientation, returns the direction from the given block
index to the transition.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRegionCombination.getDirectionRelativeToRegion
documentation:
From the perspective of the canonical orientation of the region
specified by the given index, returns the direction of this transition.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.PathFindingRegionCombination.getExpectedInwardTransitions
documentation:
Returns a mapping of {point: pathStubs} for all edges that are expected
to go through this combination from either side, based on the
departing edges recorded in the neighbouring regions.

documentation: Yields (ticks, actionList, stationaryPoint)

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.StationaryPoint.inwardEdges
documentation: Yields (ticks, actionList, stationaryPoint)

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.StationaryPoint.blocksAndCombos
documentation:
Yields (blockDef, halfCombo) for each combination that this point
can reach, or that can reach this point.

methods.name: trosnoth.bots.pathfinding.StationaryPoint.getCostsToWaypoints
documentation:

return: |-
  dict of concreteWaypoint: ticks for all waypoints for which
  this point has precalculated costs.
---

Used as intermediate points in pathfinding.
